text: I am not in danger, Skyler. I am the danger.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt asserts dominance during escalating pressure at home.

text: Yeah, Mr. White! Yeah, science!
speaker: Jesse Pinkman

text: I hide in plain sight, same as you.
speaker: Gustavo Fring
contextNote: Gus reveals parallels between his facade and Walt's life.

text: You got one part of that wrong. This is not meth.
speaker: Walter White

text: Shut the hell up and let me die in peace.
speaker: Mike Ehrmantraut

text: I'm in the empire business.
speaker: Walter White

text: A guy that clean has to be dirty.
speaker: Hank Schrader
contextNote: Hank suspects Gus Fring's public image.

text: I used to be a beat cop. Now I'm just a guy who knows people.
speaker: Mike Ehrmantraut
contextNote: Mike shares a glimpse into his past.

text: Say my name.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Demand for recognition during a desert standoff.

text: Magnets, b****!
speaker: Jesse Pinkman
contextNote: Jesse's plan for the evidence room.

text: Stay out of my territory.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt marks his turf at a hardware store.

text: They’re minerals, Marie!
speaker: Hank Schrader

text: I will kill your infant daughter.
speaker: Gustavo Fring

text: No more half measures.
speaker: Mike Ehrmantraut

text: Tread lightly.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: A warning to Hank in the garage.

text: Cow house? Where they live... the cows.
speaker: Jesse Pinkman

text: I did it for me. I was good at it.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt finally admits his true motivation.

text: Never make the same mistake twice.
speaker: Gustavo Fring

text: You’re the smartest guy I ever met, but you're too stupid to see.
speaker: Hank Schrader

text: I’m a blowfish!
speaker: Jesse Pinkman

text: A man provides. And he does it even when he's not appreciated.
speaker: Gustavo Fring
contextNote: Gus manipulates Walt's sense of duty.

text: You are a time bomb.
speaker: Mike Ehrmantraut

text: Run.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt saves Jesse from the dealers.

text: I'm a manufacturer, not a dealer.
speaker: Walter White

text: Rice 'n Beans?
speaker: Jesse Pinkman

text: Chemistry is the study of change.
speaker: Walter White

text: We're done when I say we're done.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt asserting power over his associates.

text: My name is ASAC Schrader, and you can go f*** yourself.
speaker: Hank Schrader

text: I’m a private domicile and I will not be searched, b****!
speaker: Jesse Pinkman
contextNote: Jesse quotes the law to Hank.

text: Just because you shot Jesse James, don't make you Jesse James.
speaker: Mike Ehrmantraut

text: Who are you talking to right now?
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Lead up to the danger speech.

text: The business is my life.
speaker: Gustavo Fring

text: He's the devil. He is luckier than you.
speaker: Jesse Pinkman

text: I wish I could have seen it.
speaker: Hank Schrader

text: I have spent my whole life scared.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt reflects on fear vs action.

text: You want to cook? Let's cook.
speaker: Walter White

text: Is this a bad time?
speaker: Todd Alquist

text: I found it. I found the pool.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt in a deep state of depression.

text: I'm the guy who killed Gus Fring.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt uses his reputation as a weapon.

text: What good is being an outlaw when you have responsibilities?
speaker: Jesse Pinkman

text: Everything I did, I did for this family.
speaker: Walter White

text: Do you really want to live in a world without Coca-Cola?
speaker: Gustavo Fring
contextNote: Gus uses a strange business metaphor.

text: You’re my free pass, b****!
speaker: Jesse Pinkman

text: I'm an extremely cautious man.
speaker: Gustavo Fring

text: Executive delivery boy.
speaker: Jesse Pinkman
contextNote: Jesse describes his new job with Mike.

text: I’m the one who knocks!
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: The climax of the danger speech.

text: Whatever you think is supposed to happen, the exact reverse is going to happen.
speaker: Mike Ehrmantraut

text: I have a family! I have a son!
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt pleading for his life in the pilot.

text: I'm not leaving without my money.
speaker: Jesse Pinkman

text: Dipping sticks, Skyler.
speaker: Walter White
contextNote: Walt tries to act normal while hiding secrets.
